title: Immunolabeling of endogenous proteins for fluorescence microscopy in cultured mammalian cells
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"knwn2","text":"This protocol details the detection of endogenous proteins by immunofluorescence. This is our standard protocol for imaging endogenous proteins associated with mitochondria and other membrane-bound organelles.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"b6r9u","text":"Introduction","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":12}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bpf36","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dgcbc","text":"This protocol provides detailed instructions for detection of endogenous proteins in fixed cells by immunofluorescence (Immunofluorescence Assay/IFA). The protocol results in a fluorescent sample, to be visualized using a high-resolution fluorescence confocal microscope. Target proteins appear as distinct signals corresponding to the fluorophore-conjugated secondary antibodies, allowing the spatial analysis of subcellular structures, protein interactions, and nucleic acids.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"cas5y","text":"Proliferating cancer cell line or fibroblasts at 40% confluency","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"mni0v","text":"1X Phosphate-buffered saline (PBS), pH 7.4","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"imigs","text":"Fixative: 4% Paraformaldehyde in PBS, pH 7.4","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"qpetw","text":"Quenching Solution: 1M Glycine in PBS, pH 7.4","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"rm41v","text":"Permeabilization Solution: 0.1% Triton-X 100 in PBS, pH 7.4","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bavea","text":"Blocking Solution: 0.1% Tween in 1X TBS + 1% BSA (Bovine Serum Albumin)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5jlfj","text":"Glass bottom poly-D-lysine-coated dishes (P35GC-1.5-14-C, MatTek)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"qbscx","text":"Primary antibody (specific to the target protein)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"svuac","text":"Secondary antibody conjugated to fluorophore of choice","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Prepare the sample by plating cells in 2 mL of media at a 20% density in a glass bottom poly-D-lysine-coated dish (P35GC-1.5-14-C, MatTek).
Grow cells for 24:00:00 until they reach a confluency of approximately 40%.
Aspirate media and fix the cells with 2 mL of pre-warmed 4% PFA in PBS.
Incubate for 00:20:00 at Room temperature on a shaker gently rotating.
Quench the reaction by adding 200 µL of 1M glycine in PBS to the fixed sample and incubate for 00:01:00 at Room temperature .
Aspirate the quenched fixative and wash the sample once with PBS.
Add 2 mL of 0.1% Triton X-100 in PBS and incubate for 00:10:00 at Room temperature on a shaker to permeabilize the sample.
Aspirate permeabilized solution and wash sample once with PBS.
Add 2 mL of blocking buffer (1% BSA + 0.1% Tween in 1X TBS) with a 00:10:00 incubation at Room temperature on a shaker.
Aspirate blocking buffer and add a freshly prepared 2 mL primary antibody dilution (typically a 1:1000 dilution) made in blocking buffer.
Incubate the sample Overnight at 4 °C in the dark.
Aspirate the primary antibody solution and wash the sample once with 2 mL of blocking buffer.
Aspirate wash and add 2 mL of freshly prepared secondary antibody dilution (typically at a 1:2000 dilution) with a 00:10:00 incubation at Room temperature on a shaker in the dark.
Aspirate the secondary antibody solution and wash the sample with 2 mL of blocking buffer with a 00:10:00 incubation at Room temperature on a shaker.
Remove wash and cover sample with 2 mL PBS.
Samples may be stored at 4 °C protected from light for up to 72:00:00 ; bring dishes to Room temperature before imaging.
creator: Samantha C Lewis
